source_hierarchy: Official AdonisJS v7 docs on docs.adonisjs.com and official package docs.
Existing repository conventions, but only when the wider correction is out of scope and mixing styles would be worse.
Personal preference only after the first two sources are exhausted.

protocol_steps.instruction: Choose exactly one profile: web, mixed, or api-only before designing the solution.
Read only the references needed for the selected profile and the active feature slice.
Apply every hard blocker from this manifest that matches the selected profile and task context.
Compare the request, repo conventions, and the applicable blockers against the source hierarchy before proceeding.
If a hard blocker conflicts with the request, stop, cite the blocking rule id, ask exactly one short override question, and wait.
Before finalizing, confirm the selected profile, override status, and hard-blocker compliance markers.

output_contract.instruction: Report selected-profile: <web|mixed|api-only> in the final answer.
Report override-status: none|requested|approved in the final answer.
Report hard-blocker-compliance: pass|override in the final answer.

profiles.statement: Inertia React only for pages, session or cookie auth for browser flows, and no JSON-first page architecture.
Inertia React for pages plus a separate /api route group and separate API controllers; browser clients inside the same app stay on session auth even when they call /api.
No inertia pages; use router.resource(...).apiOnly() for pure CRUD and explicit routes for everything else.

rules.statement: Use official AdonisJS packages, official setup flows, and node ace add/configure/generator commands first whenever the framework already covers the need. Do not replace official packages with third-party alternatives by default.
forbidden_patterns: third-party replacement when an official package exists
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.official-packages`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Use Lucid for SQL persistence and Luxon DateTime for model and domain dates.
forbidden_patterns: plain Date for persisted domain dates
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.data-stack`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: HTTP validation uses VineJS with vine.create(...) as the root schema and request.validateUsing(...) inside controllers. Inline controller validation and ad hoc payload parsing are not allowed.
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.validation-stack`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Use @adonisjs/auth for authentication, and keep browser-driven flows on session or cookie auth instead of bearer-token-first browser flows.
forbidden_patterns: browser bearer-token auth as the default path
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.auth-browser-stack`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Keep auth guard names fixed to web for browser sessions and api for external access tokens.
forbidden_patterns: renamed guards for the standard browser/API split
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.guard-names`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Browser-facing Inertia applications keep Shield CSRF enabled with enableXsrfCookie: true.
forbidden_patterns: disable browser CSRF for Inertia applications
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.browser-csrf`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: External clients, mobile apps, scripts, and machine-to-machine integrations use official access tokens with explicit expiration when token auth is required.
forbidden_patterns: custom token scheme for external clients
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.access-tokens-external`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: All web UI uses Inertia React and Mantine. Inertia owns navigation, so do not layer a second client router such as react-router-dom on top.
forbidden_patterns: Vue or Edge feature rendering for web UI
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.web-ui-stack`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Use @adonisjs/bouncer for authorization, @adonisjs/mail for email, and @adonisjs/drive for persistent file storage and uploads when those concerns apply.
forbidden_patterns: CASL as the default authorization path
raw mail or storage libraries when official packages cover the need
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.official-side-effect-packages`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Never mix Inertia page actions and JSON API actions in the same controller. Mixed applications keep web controllers and API controllers separate.
forbidden_patterns: single controller handling both Inertia pages and JSON endpoints
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.web-api-controller-separation`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Do not compose an AdonisJS application like Express or Fastify. Use framework-native routes, middleware, services, policies, and container primitives instead.
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.no-express-fastify-composition`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Do not add repository layers on top of Lucid for ordinary application code.
forbidden_patterns: repository wrapper over Lucid for normal feature work
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.no-repository-layer`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Do not use Edge as a feature rendering layer. The only allowed Edge file in this doctrine is the minimal resources/views/inertia_layout.edge boot layout.
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.no-edge-feature-rendering`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Do not use request.all() or request.only() as substitutes for real validation.
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.no-request-all-only`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Do not introduce any in product code, snippets, or canonical patterns.
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.no-any`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Do not use raw nodemailer, raw persistent fs calls, or ad hoc timer logic inside the HTTP runtime.
required_patterns: Ace commands or events for recurring work
forbidden_patterns: persistent fs writes in app code
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.no-raw-io-and-timers`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Do not use raw fetch, axios, ky, or SWR as the default client-side data stack for standard Inertia application work.
required_patterns: Tuyau only when explicit client fetching is justified
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.no-client-fetch-stack`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Do not use @mantine/form, react-hook-form, formik, zod, yup, or valibot as the default form stack for standard Inertia forms.
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.no-client-form-stack`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Do not default to custom API-key auth when official access tokens cover the requirement.
forbidden_patterns: custom API key auth as the default path
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.no-custom-api-keys-default`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Use @adonisjs/queue for background job processing. Define jobs as classes extending Job<T> with typed payloads, dispatch via JobClass.dispatch(payload), and run workers with node ace queue:work. Do not use raw BullMQ, bee-queue, agenda, or ad hoc Redis polling as the default queue path.
forbidden_patterns: raw BullMQ as the default queue
rules.conflict_behavior: Stop, cite `hb.queue-stack`, ask one override question, and wait for explicit one-off confirmation.

rules.statement: Resolve design choices with this order: official docs and packages first, then repository conventions when the broader correction is out of scope, then personal preference. Never invent local conventions without reading the repo.
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply this default hierarchy unless a hard blocker forces an explicit stop.

rules.statement: Select one canonical profile before implementation. Web means Inertia-only pages. Mixed means separate web and /api surfaces. API-only means no inertia pages. Browser clients inside the same app stay on session auth even when they call /api.
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the selected profile before choosing architecture details.

rules.statement: Follow the canonical build order unless a step is irrelevant: official package coverage, env/config, migration, model, validator, policy, service, transformer, side effects, controller, routes, tests, then UI.
forbidden_patterns: UI-first feature implementation without backend contract
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the canonical order as the default feature sequence.

rules.statement: Use framework-native routing and kernel structure: router from @adonisjs/core/services/router, named routes, route helpers, separate web and /api groups, resource routes only for standard CRUD, and named middleware registered in start/kernel.ts.
forbidden_patterns: hardcoded route strings when helpers exist
inline auth logic in route handlers
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply routing and middleware defaults unless repo-local conventions already match a compatible alternative.

rules.statement: Shared Inertia props live in app/middleware/inertia_middleware.ts. The middleware extends BaseInertiaMiddleware from @adonisjs/inertia/inertia_middleware and stays minimal: user (via UserTransformer), flash, errors, and app { name, env } only. Augment SharedProps through declare module '@adonisjs/inertia/types'.
forbidden_patterns: lists, counters, filters, or page data in shared props
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the minimal shared-props contract by default.

rules.statement: Business logic lives in app/services with canonical verbs like list, findOrFail, create, update, and delete. Services take primitive ids unless a preloaded model is required for policy checks and return domain objects instead of HTTP payloads.
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply service-layer defaults when business logic or orchestration is required.

rules.statement: Use one validator file per resource or workflow and one compiled validator per action. Prefer small duplication over clever validator inheritance.
forbidden_patterns: validator inheritance as the default pattern
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply explicit action validators by default.

rules.statement: Keep relations, scopes, hooks, and date semantics in models. Use one policy per protected resource, prefer explicit denies(...) checks in controllers, and default authorization failures to 403 instead of 404.
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply explicit model and policy boundaries by default.

rules.statement: Use dedicated mail classes, class-based events and listeners for secondary side effects, one transformer per resource, and named exceptions in app/exceptions with flat API { code, message } mapping and web redirect+flash handling.
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the mail, event, transformer, and exception defaults whenever those concerns appear.

rules.statement: Runtime configuration lives in config/* and every new env variable must be declared in start/env.ts with type, required/optional state, defaults, and allowed values before feature code uses it.
forbidden_patterns: process.env access in controllers or services
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply config/env defaults before feature code reads new settings.

rules.statement: Keep config/auth.ts, config/session.ts, config/inertia.ts, and config/shield.ts aligned with the doctrine: web is the default guard, remember-me tokens are optional and stay at 30 days, sessions clear with the browser, cookies stay conservative, SSR is off by default, and browser Inertia apps keep the XSRF cookie enabled.
forbidden_patterns: session persistence stretched to simulate remember me
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply auth, session, inertia, and shield config defaults unless the product explicitly overrides one of them.

rules.statement: Put request flows in tests/functional and isolated logic in tests/unit. Inertia form flows assert redirects and flash/session effects; API routes assert JSON payload shape, canonical status codes, and auth behavior.
forbidden_patterns: missing request-flow coverage for user-facing mutations
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the functional/unit split and the canonical assertions by default.

rules.statement: Use the canonical Inertia filesystem layout: inertia/pages, inertia/components, inertia/layouts, inertia/stores, inertia/theme.ts, inertia/app.tsx, optional inertia/ssr.tsx when SSR is enabled, and optional inertia/client.ts when explicit client fetching is justified. Export page components in PascalCase and keep business logic on the server.
forbidden_patterns: business logic moved into React components
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the canonical Inertia file layout by default.

rules.statement: Default to Mantine core, Mantine notifications, Tabler icons, and @adonisjs/inertia/react Link and Form wrappers. Always use Mantine components (Button, TextInput, Select, Card, Stack, Group, Modal, Menu, Table, etc.) instead of raw HTML elements when Mantine provides an equivalent. Use raw HTML or a specialized third-party component only when the UI need is genuinely complex, requires deep customization, and no Mantine component covers the requirement. Use @inertiajs/react only for boot-time primitives not provided by the Adonis wrapper. React local state, useReducer, and useRemember are the default state tools. Zustand is for shared UI-only state. @mantine/dates requires dayjs. Use Tuyau for explicit client fetching, TanStack Query only when truly needed, QueryClientProvider only when used, and TanStack Table only for advanced grids.
required_patterns: Tuyau when explicit client fetching is needed
Mantine components instead of raw HTML when an equivalent exists
forbidden_patterns: raw HTML elements (div, input, button, select) when a Mantine equivalent exists
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the canonical frontend library and state policy unless a repo-local standard already matches an allowed alternative.

rules.statement: Use node ace generators for controllers, validators, services, policies, mails, events, listeners, commands, transformers, and tests. Keep canonical naming for route names, policy abilities, services, validators, and command names. Reference controllers through the generated barrel: import { controllers } from '#generated/controllers' then use the PascalCase resource name (controllers.Posts), not a suffixed alias.
forbidden_patterns: manual scaffolding when a generator exists
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply generator-first naming defaults unless the repository already standardizes a compatible variant.

rules.statement: AdonisJS v7 requires Node.js 24 or higher and npm 11 or higher. Use npm create adonisjs@latest my-app -- --kit=<hypermedia|react|vue|api> to scaffold new applications. Start the dev server with node ace serve --hmr for development.
forbidden_patterns: npm init adonisjs@latest as the canonical scaffold command
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the v7 runtime prerequisites as the default environment contract.

rules.statement: adonisrc.ts declares the v7 hooks pipeline under hooks.init: indexEntities() from @adonisjs/core is mandatory. Inertia apps add indexPages({ framework: 'react' }) from @adonisjs/inertia plus generateRegistry() from @tuyau/core/hooks and indexEntities({ transformers: { enabled: true, withSharedProps: true } }). Bouncer apps add indexPolicies() from @adonisjs/bouncer. Vite apps add @adonisjs/vite/build_hook under buildStarting. These hooks generate #generated/controllers, @generated/data, @generated/registry, and the typed shared-props surface.
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the canonical hooks pipeline whenever Inertia, Tuyau, Bouncer, or Vite are in use.

rules.statement: Serialize API success payloads with transformers through the framework serialize(...) helper. The helper produces the canonical AdonisJS v7 envelope ({ data: ... } for resources, paginator shape for lists); do not wrap it in a second custom envelope. Use flat { code, message } domain errors and canonical statuses: 201 create, 200 update, 204 delete, 403 forbidden, 404 not found, 409 conflict.
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the canonical API payload defaults unless a surrounding contract already requires a compatible alternative.

rules.statement: For non-trivial list endpoints, create a dedicated query validator and validate page, perPage, q, sort, direction, and business filters. Default perPage to 20 and cap it at 100.
forbidden_patterns: query parsing in controllers without a validator
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the canonical query and pagination defaults by default.

rules.statement: Create a service for non-trivial writes and orchestration. Open managed transactions in services when two or more writes must succeed together. Keep external I/O outside the transaction when possible. Use listeners for secondary side effects. Use Ace commands for imports, exports, backfills, recurring jobs, cleanup, syncs, and operator tasks, and keep recurring commands idempotent.
forbidden_patterns: external I/O inside long transactions by default
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply service, transaction, side-effect, and command defaults when the use case coordinates more than one dependency.

rules.statement: Web mutations default to redirect plus flash/session feedback. JSON responses from web controllers are the exception. In mixed apps, browser-called /api endpoints stay on session auth unless an external client requirement exists. API-only apps serving external clients default to official access tokens.
required_patterns: official access tokens for external clients
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply the web mutation and browser/API auth defaults unless an explicit product requirement says otherwise.

rules.statement: Keep controllers thin and observable: no direct DB orchestration, Mail, Drive, transactions, or durable business logic in controllers beyond simple request coordination.
rules.conflict_behavior: Note this preference when controller boundaries drift, but do not stop unless a hard blocker is also violated.

rules.statement: Prefer AdonisJS services, middleware, URL builders, test helpers, fakes, and container features over generic Node.js patterns when both options are viable.
forbidden_patterns: generic Node.js pattern chosen without need
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply this preference when it materially improves consistency, but do not stop unless a hard blocker is also involved.

rules.statement: Prefer small, explicit duplication over clever abstractions in validators, controllers, and support code when the abstraction would obscure the doctrine.
forbidden_patterns: generic helpers.ts or utils.ts with no bounded purpose
rules.conflict_behavior: Note this preference when abstractions become diffuse, but do not stop unless a harder rule is also involved.

rules.statement: If you are unsure whether logic belongs in a service, create the service. The doctrine prefers service extraction over controller expansion for ambiguous cases.
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply this tie-breaker when structure is ambiguous.

rules.statement: Keep the doctrine observable: prefer rule ids, profile markers, and compliance markers in prompts, wrappers, and evaluations so drift can be tested quickly.
rules.conflict_behavior: Apply this observability preference when maintaining the skill pack itself.

rules.statement: Before using a package API for the first time in a session, read the exported types or source to confirm the exact signature. AdonisJS ecosystem packages (@adonisjs/inertia/react Link and Form, Tuyau urlFor and client, pdfkit, @adonisjs/mail attach) have specific APIs that LLMs frequently hallucinate. Guessing a signature and fixing it later costs more than reading the type first.
required_patterns: verify type signature before first use
forbidden_patterns: guessed API signatures without reading types
rules.conflict_behavior: Note this preference when integrating a package API. Do not stop execution, but prefer reading types over guessing.
